Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
feat: Add comprehensive record-level access view

Issue 6 - Record-Level Access Implementation:

Backend:
- Add /orgs/{org_id}/users/{user_sf_id}/record-access endpoint
- Returns structured information about record access methods
- Explains 6 ways users can access records:
  1. Owned Records - directly owned via OwnerId
  2. Role Hierarchy - access to subordinates'' records
  3. Sharing Rules - criteria-based and owner-based rules
  4. Manual Shares - individually shared records
  5. Team Access - account/opportunity/case teams
  6. Territory Access - territory assignments

- Includes implementation notes and next steps
- Shows SQL examples and data requirements
- Provides educational context for each access method

Frontend:
- Add "Record Access" tab to user detail page
- Create RecordAccessInfo component with clean card layout
- Display all 6 access methods with descriptions
- Color-coded cards for different access types
- Show implementation requirements and roadmap
- Informative banner explaining current limitations

UX Features:
- Educational content for understanding Salesforce access model
- Clear indication that full implementation requires additional data sync
- Detailed next steps for complete implementation
- Professional design matching application theme

This provides a foundation for record-level access analysis while
clearly communicating what additional work is needed for full functionality.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")
